# ClearDesk

> Free ADHD tools and honest guides for work, daily life, and your brain. Built from real experience, not a textbook.

ClearDesk is a collection of free browser-based tools and long-form guides for people with ADHD. No account required. No paywall. Tools store data locally in the browser only.

## Tools

- [Body Double Timer](/body-double-timer): Focus timer with customizable intervals for ADHD brains. Pomodoro-compatible but not Pomodoro-locked. Tracks sessions with a visual dot counter.
- [Meds Tracker](/meds-tracker): One-tap daily medication log. Tracks streaks. Shows a 28-day history grid. Fully offline, data stays in localStorage.
- [Task Unkicker](/task-unkicker): Paste any stuck task. AI breaks it into 5-7 physical, specific steps small enough to start. Uses Gemini 2.5 Flash via a server-side API route.
- [ADHD Job Fit Checker](/adhd-job-fit): Eight questions about a role. Scores structural compatibility with ADHD brains across environment, task variety, feedback loops, and interruption tolerance.
- [Medication Comparison](/medication-comparison): Community-reported patterns for Adderall XR, Vyvanse, Ritalin, Strattera, and Concerta. Sort by symptom priority. No clinical claims.
- [Screen Break Timer](/screen-break): Enforced screen break timer with doomscroll counter. Helps interrupt the phone loop that ADHD brains are particularly susceptible to.
- [Overwhelmed Right Now](/overwhelmed): A minimal crisis landing page with a 5-4-3-2-1 grounding exercise, a 5-minute body double timer, and a direct path to the task unkicker. No nav distractions.
- [Just Diagnosed Guide](/just-diagnosed): A plain guide for people newly diagnosed with ADHD. Covers what the diagnosis means, why grief is common, what to expect from medication, and what to stop feeling guilty about.

## Articles

- [ADHD-Friendly Jobs](/articles/adhd-friendly-jobs): What people with ADHD actually switched to and why those roles work structurally.
- [The 2-Minute Rule](/articles/two-minute-rule): Why this specific productivity rule cuts through task paralysis when bigger systems fail.
- [AuDHD in an Open Office](/articles/audHD-open-office): Sensory overload, accommodations, and what actually helps in open-plan workplaces.
- [Email Paralysis](/articles/email-paralysis): Strategies beyond unsubscribing, from people who have had thousands of unread messages.
- [Vyvanse vs Adderall](/articles/vyvanse-vs-adderall): Community-reported patterns from people who have taken both medications.
- [ADHD Memory and Relationships](/articles/adhd-memory-relationships): What the clinical articles miss about how memory issues affect partnerships.
- [ADHD Sleep](/articles/adhd-sleep): Why ADHD sleep problems differ from regular insomnia, including delayed sleep phase, racing thoughts, and medication timing.
- [Doomscrolling](/articles/doomscrolling): Why the phone loop is harder to break with ADHD and what actually interrupts it.
- [Exercise and ADHD](/articles/adhd-exercise): The initiation problem is the whole problem. What people report actually working for consistent exercise.
- [Eating with ADHD](/articles/adhd-eating): Appetite suppression from medication, executive dysfunction around cooking, and forgetting to eat entirely.

## About

ClearDesk is an independent site. Not affiliated with any pharmaceutical company or medical provider. Content describes community-reported experiences and is not medical advice.

## Contact

Available via the [contact page](/contact).
